title: betula lenta var. grossa
intro: Betula lenta var. grossa, also known as the American yellow birch, is a variety of tree that belongs to the Betulaceae family and is native to eastern North America. Traditionally, the bark of the American yellow birch has been used in herbal medicine due to its antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties. The medicinal properties of Betula lenta var. grossa are attributed to its ability to reduce fever, alleviate pain, and soothe digestive issues, making it beneficial for health conditions such as colds, flu, and menstrual cramps. In herbal preparations, the bark is often used in teas, tinctures, and infusions, and it can also be used to make salves and ointments for topical application.
